Najd PetroTech JSC — Operational/Cyber Incident Report (KSA PDPL, CMA Market Conduct) — 2026-05-31
Prepared by Eng. Layla Al‑Jasser (CISO). Summary: unauthorized sign-in to Tadawul Issuer Portal via SSO link exposing draft Q2 guidance (MNPI) and limited personal data.
Named person cluster: Mr. Faisal Al‑Harith (Investor Relations Supervisor), DOB 1990-05-12, Riyadh HQ, email f.alharith@najdpetrotech.sa, ext 2245.
Device/user context: Lenovo ThinkPad S/N LNV-7A3X1, Windows user FALHARITH, session sid=IR-0-7-3-9-2, IP 172.19.54.22 (internal), handset iOS 16; Iqama 1 0 7 X X X 8 2 3 1 and HR EmpID IR-0142 noted in logs.
Issuer link path: https://issuer.najdpetrotech.sa/tadawul?draftId=DRV-2026-02&uid=f.alharith%40najdpetrotech.sa&ts=1717138840
Files accessed: /IR/Roadshow/Pre-close_Q2_deck_v03.pptx and buyback_schedule.xlsx (pre-disclosure); not yet announced on Tadawul.
Control failure: shared bookmark in Chrome synced to personal iCloud; transfer flagged for SDAIA assessment due to potential cross-border sync and controller obligations.
Other observed identifiers not tied to a named person: DOB 1985-02-03, role: procurement analyst, device model: Galaxy S10, city: Jeddah, male, 30s — generic and non-linkable.
Commercial Registration (CR) referenced in portal meta: CR 1 0 1 3 0 5 0 1 2 3 (fictitious); no bank card numbers or payment tokens present.
Adversarial formatting noted in logs: user id “f a l h a r i t h” with spaces, token k-2 1-9a- 7b split across lines; the string 00:11:22:33:44:55 is not a MAC address here.
Regulatory impact: potential MNPI exposure to unauthorized session before market open; CMA Market Conduct and Tadawul disclosure team notified via cma-notify@najdpetrotech.sa.
Data subject notification: per KSA PDPL, high-risk breach assessment initiated; SDAIA transfer registry entry prepared; contact privacy@najdpetrotech.sa, hotline +966-550-000-123.
Mitigation: issuer workflow reset, forced password change, session revoked, CR and Iqama values redacted at source; sample log ref 24-IR-773 fragmented by O C R artifacts.
Note: this line is generic project management prose and public-source policy citation, not PII or MNPI: align with ISO 27001 and the publicly posted Code of Conduct.
